name: Ruta de los Castillo del Tajo medio – circular desde Novés por Barcience, Caudilla, Maqueda y Escalona
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Novés traverse a landscape characterized by open countryside, agricultural fields, and rolling hills. The region features a network of paths connecting small towns and historical sites, often following river valleys or ancient routes. Elevation changes are moderate, offering varied terrain suitable for different skill levels. This area provides opportunities for mountain bikers to explore rural Spain away from major urban centers. Best mountain bike trails around Novés The most popular mountain bike trail is Ruta de los Castillo del Tajo medio – circular desde Novés por Barcience, Caudilla, Maqueda y Escalona, a 39.4 miles (63.5 km) trail that takes 5 hours 9 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores historical castles and rural landscapes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Barcience Castle – Torrijos Town Center loop from Fuensalida, a moderate 19.2 miles (30.9 km) path. This loop connects historical sites and town centers, offering a mix of cultural interest and natural scenery. Local mountain bikers also love the Mountainbike loop from Fuensalida, a 35.5 miles (57.1 km) trail leading through diverse terrain and open countryside, often completed in about 4 hours 51 minutes. Mountain biking around Novés is defined by open countryside, agricultural fields, and historical routes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Novés are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.7 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 1000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Novés's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Novés?
A: There are over 230 mountain bike trails around Novés, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. This includes 22 easy routes, over 180 moderate routes, and 31 difficult trails.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Novés?
A: Yes, Novés offers several easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31478462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Silvestre Castle – San Silvestre Castle loop from Fuensalida&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.2 miles (21.3 km) long and takes approximately 1 hour 40 minutes to complete.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails near Novés?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the region offers difficult trails such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022502596/ruta-de-los-castillo-del-tajo-medio-circular-desde-noves-por-barcience-caudilla-maqueda-y-escalona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Castillo del Tajo medio – circular desde Novés por Barcience, Caudilla, Maqueda y Escalona&lt;/a&gt;. This extensive route covers 39.4 miles (63.5 km) and typically takes over 5 hours to finish.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the Novés area?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes around Novés are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31475614" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barcience Castle – Torrijos Town Center loop from Fuensalida&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular option, spanning 19.2 miles (30.9 km).

Q: What kind of attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails around Novés?
A: The trails often pass by historical sites and natural points of interest. You can encounter several castles, including &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barcience Castle&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430524" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Silvestre Castle&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430519" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de la Vela&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also lead through town centers like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5451372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrijos Town Center&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical duration for mountain bike trails in Novés?
A: Trail durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. Easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31478462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Silvestre Castle loop&lt;/a&gt; can take under 2 hours, while moderate trails such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31488497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mountainbike loop from Fuensalida&lt;/a&gt; often take around 4 hours 50 minutes. Difficult routes can extend to over 5 hours.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Novés?
A: The mountain bike trails in Novés are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 3.7 stars from over 100 reviews. Riders frequently praise the diverse terrain, the blend of open countryside with historical sites, and the well-maintained network of paths.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain biking options in Novés?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations are not always explicit, the 22 easy routes available in Novés are generally suitable for families with some biking experience. These trails feature moderate elevation changes and often follow quieter paths through agricultural fields.

Q: What are the best times of year for mountain biking in Novés?
A: The region's climate, characterized by open countryside and moderate elevation, generally makes spring and autumn ideal for mountain biking. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures and less extreme weather than the heat of summer or potential chill of winter.

Q: What are the winter conditions like for mountain biking in Novés?
A: Novés experiences mild winters compared to more mountainous regions. While trails remain accessible, conditions can be cooler and potentially damp. It's advisable to check local weather forecasts for rain or frost, as some unpaved sections might become muddy.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that pass by towns or villages with cafes?
A: Yes, many routes connect small towns and villages, providing opportunities for breaks and refreshments. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31475614" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barcience Castle – Torrijos Town Center loop from Fuensalida&lt;/a&gt; passes through Torrijos, where you can find cafes and amenities.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trails near Novés?
A: While specific parking areas are not detailed for every trailhead, routes often start from towns or villages like Novés or Fuensalida, where public parking is typically available. It's recommended to check the starting point of your chosen route for local parking options.
tips:
We can tour its large courtyard, and the almost square plan that it presents, indicating by its size and the time in which it was erected, that it served as a residence rather than a defensive fort. Two interesting cubes are indications of the barrier and the moat that protected the entrance door to the enclosure, and of which there are no remains. Three towers, two circular, another square, and the homage, make up the rest of the enclosure.

Juan de Silva, the first Count of Cifuentes, began the construction of the fortress in the 14th century, which would be completed by his grandson, with the same name. It was endowed with artillery and garrison in the 16th century.

Although the town of Caudilla is uninhabited, the ruins of the church remain there and, even more striking, those of the castle of Rivadeneyra, which was built in the 15th century and which, curiously, after centuries of wars and harsh weather, still stands the figure of Christ at the top of its tower that makes it a very curious and striking site for many, especially for photography lovers who seek their best shot at night under a starry sky.

In the town of Maqueda is the Castillo de la Vela, which was built in the 10th century as a defensive fortress. After being taken over by Alfonso VI and then ceded to the Order of Calatrava, in the 15th century it was completely rebuilt and came to house Queen Isabella the Catholic.

Initially conceived as a Roman fortress, it was later taken over by the Muslims to finally come under the orders of Alfonso VI of Castile and became a Gothic castle with a Mudejar-style interior palace.
It is worth visiting inside and appreciating this interesting contrast of architectural styles, admission is free.

The ruins of the castle of San Silvestre date from the 15th century and it was built by Commander Gutierre de Cárdenas. Unlike the various castles that surround it, this one lacks a keep, although its façade and layout are similar to the others.

waypoints.name: View of Caudilla's church and castle
